competency_groups.title: Sets and Functions Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Sets, Relations & Functions, and Trigonometric Functions.
competencies: Represent sets using various methods including empty set, finite/infinite sets, equal sets, subsets, intervals (with notations), universal set, Venn diagrams, union, intersection, difference, complement, and properties of complement.
Define relations as ordered pairs with domain, co-domain, and range; define functions as special types of relations with pictorial representation.
Analyze real-valued functions including constant, identity, polynomial, rational, modulus, signum, exponential, logarithmic, and greatest integer functions along with their graphs.
Perform operations on functions: sum, difference, product, and quotients.
Measure angles in radians and degrees; convert between measures using the unit circle definition of trigonometric functions.
Verify fundamental identities such as sin²x + cos²x = 1 for all x.
Determine signs, domain, range, and graphs of trigonometric functions.
Express sin(x ± y) and cos(x ± y) in terms of individual components and deduce related sum-to-product/product-to-sum identities (tan, cot variations).
Derive and apply double angle formulas for sin 2x, cos 2x, tan 2x and triple angle formulas for sin 3x, cos 3x, tan 3x.
Solve general trigonometric equations of the type siny = sina, cosy = cosa, tany = tana.

competency_groups.title: Algebra Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Complex Numbers, Quadratic Equations, Linear Inequalities, Permutations & Combinations, Binomial Theorem, and Sequence & Series.
competencies: Motivate the need for complex numbers (specifically √-1) due to unsolvable quadratic equations in real systems; apply algebraic properties of complex numbers.
Represent complex numbers on the Argand plane including polar representation.
State and apply the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra to solve quadratic equations with real coefficients in the complex number system.
Solve linear inequalities algebraically in one variable and represent solutions on a number line.
Graphical solution of linear inequalities in two variables; graphical method for systems of linear inequalities in two variables.
Apply the fundamental principle of counting, factorial notation (n!), and derive formulae for Permutations (Pr) and Combinations (Cr).
Solve problems involving permutations and combinations with simple applications.
State and prove the Binomial Theorem for positive integral indices; utilize Pascal's triangle for expansion properties.
Identify general and middle terms in binomial expansions.
Analyze sequences and series including Arithmetic Mean (A.M.) and Geometric Progression (G.P.).
Calculate the general term of a G.P., sum of n terms, infinite G.P. sums, geometric mean (G.M.), and relations between A.M. and G.M.

competency_groups.title: Coordinate Geometry Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Straight Lines, Conic Sections, and Three-dimensional Geometry.
competencies: Recall two-dimensional geometry concepts including slope of a line and angle between lines.
Derive and apply various forms of linear equations: parallel to axis, point-slope, slope-intercept, two-point form, intercept form, normal form, and general equation.
Calculate the distance of a point from a line using derived formulae.
Identify sections of a cone (circles, ellipse, parabola, hyperbola) including degenerate cases (point, straight line, intersecting lines).
Apply standard equations and simple properties for parabolas, ellipses, hyperbolas, and circles.

competency_groups.title: Three-dimensional Geometry Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to coordinate axes, planes, points, and distance in 3D.
competencies: Define coordinate axes and coordinate planes in three dimensions.
Determine the coordinates of a point in space.
Calculate the section formula for dividing line segments internally or externally.

competency_groups.title: Calculus Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Limits and Derivatives.
competencies: Understand the intuitive idea of limits for polynomials, rational functions, trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions.
Define derivative as rate of change (distance function) and geometrically (tangent slope).
Calculate derivatives of sums, differences, products, and quotients of polynomial and trigonometric functions.
Apply the Chain rule for derivatives of composite functions.

competency_groups.title: Statistics and Probability Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Measures of Dispersion, Random Experiments, and Axiomatic Probability.
competencies: Calculate measures of dispersion: range, mean deviation, variance, and standard deviation for ungrouped and grouped data.
Define random experiments, outcomes, sample space (set representation), events, 'not', 'and', 'or' events, exhaustive events, and mutually exclusive events.
